commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human review is needed because the external PR lacks real behavior proof and the remaining blockers involve auth/storage safety and contributor-run Keychain verification.; Needs attention: The diff adds a direct Keychain secret read that bypasses CodexBar's opt-out and no-UI safety path.; Review finding: [P1] Read the OAuth file before the Keychain fallback — Sources/CodexBarCore/Providers/Antigravity/Antigra... (sha=721b1eb363527b0d2f957e36fd57f6e15402beaa)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Antigravity CLI process-name detection and a macOS Keychain credential reader for the `gemini`/`antigravity` item, plus status-probe tests for CLI command variations.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was run; source inspection shows current main only loads `oauth_creds.json` and only detects `language_server` command lines. I did not run live Keychain or provider probes because repository policy forbids prompt-prone validation without explicit request.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the external PR lacks real behavior proof and the remaining blockers involve auth/storage safety and contributor-run Keychain verification.

target.title: Fix Antigravity CLI usage tracking via Keychain fallback
target.body: Since the main entry point for Antigravity is now `antigravity-cli` which stores credentials in the OS Keychain instead of `~/.codexbar/antigravity/oauth_creds.json`, usage tracking broke. This PR adds a fallback to `AntigravityOAuthCredentialsStore` to load credentials from the keychain using the `gemini` service and `antigravity` account, allowing CodexBar to successfully fetch CLI quota.
target.author: oyaah
